h439fab73ge893g48f8ga150gbcbaf4b8a003: Ethel's gone, isn't she? I felt it.
h90cad962gba9fg4228gb1begd27c01cf6547: Yes. She's left this place for good.
heecdf01ag20e7g4c6cg82d8g6dd18206a95c: I can't believe I was so stupid.
h7c66df88g329ag4ee6g95c7g531cf52f0b5f: Awake or asleep I could hear her. Laughing at me. Taunting me. Now it's gone.
h1b78256eg00b0g47f2g863bg55135eb94cc6: I'm so stupid. How could I have trusted her?
h2e8dd3dag686cg4760gb26ag9a09c047dcdb: I almost gave that monster my child.
h0d609c48g3a90g42c0gbf28g1b8080c154f8: Why the hells would you even consider that?
he7beb1e1g71cag427fg8555g53cb87da31f4: That's what she wanted - my baby. And, gods, I was going to do it - for him.
hd5f959e4gcba6g496bgb790gd40b754194c2: My husband. She was going to bring him back. Bring Connor back to life.
h957f4836g3d2cg47e6gb8feg96e4e1104072: Your husband must have meant a great deal to you.
h21f200f2g664bg4341g95b5gac07a649b133: Connor would have done anything to save me - I had to do the same.
hf44fc3dfg3d08g431cgbc6agde21cec34f27: I just wanted everything back - back the way it was.
h19bfda5bg7241g4ee2g9e4fgdb0bc27ae5c2: Ethel saw you were vulnerable. She took advantage.
hca039c48gd040g4b60gb0c5g72d4ba01fad4: It's my own fault for letting her.
heaead071g1b12g4163g9722g5a3089602963: That doesn't excuse the deal you made.
hf64deefbg9e39g4367g9f41gcd785b173127: I never said it did! I just wanted you to understand.
hc9d99083g12e7g4dbfgb6f3gce8690c48bef: I imagine Ethel thinks the same.
hd3a02299geae4g4c22g9e78gbb63fc3d489c: She always told me I was worthless.
hc7c5c01ag196ag43cbg9268gdc46b906e7d2: Look - I don't like owing people.
hab8f7fb9ge65dg4cf3ga61eg61bbc04c4195: Here. This locket is worth some coin.
hc3d73946g5377g4deag9b59g06544dc04c1a: I imagine Ethel would've said the same.
ha8cef6e3ga8aeg499cg8f00g2ba60d700a3b: What would have happened to your child?
hb643f1edg0cfeg4e64g8b6bg85ae2d997321: Ethel promised to raise the child - teach them magic. Give them a good life. That's a damn sight better than I can do.
h21757a2ag0822g4bd9g844agac38811bf6e7: You'd have sacrificed a new life for a lost life? Pure selfishness.
hfe5feca7gc149g4ca8g85cdgd2aed477b915: Let me know when you're done whining.
h308ef063g1386g488cgab01g4632a6386eeb: I'm more the fool for getting upset. It's likely gold you want, right?
hb20b2394g01d1g4d38g8f24g19d82ccb3da3: It was part of the deal. For him.
h3a889facga61bg4b57gba4eg45c6f30f3082: You bastard! You ruined it - you ruined everything!
h62301457g05d5g4e9dg960bg06691f536623: First my brothers and now my husband? What more do you want?
h1eeb80c3g51b1g439bg99cfg7eb5c3475e19: Slow down - what did I do?
h4e445a25g2b3dg4b08gb301ga564448d2b81: Ethel was going to bring my husband back. Back from the dead! And now I'll never see him again - because of you.
h98355a10gfac6g4336ga74fg3b6c25c37ec5: Because of Auntie Ethel. You nearly died!
h3d2d215eg1310g4bfag9cb8g131ea9caca69: I was fine. She wouldn't have killed me - I had something she wanted.
h14101383g86ccg422dg92bdg12a43ad43e50: Just a bit longer and my child would have been born. And all this - all this would have been over.
h52603e8eg7337g49c5g8a51g06ae52ad0468: You were going to trade your baby for your dead husband?
h36bb5f5bg1766g4f7fg9641gddef3de3bc12: Don't judge me. I have nothing. My baby would have been raised in rags.
h6e19349bg7cf8g4c14gae19g18ab874190c6: Auntie Ethel promised to give this child a good life - teach them magic, even!
h2fcb7c77g4f71g4167ga942gf528ab2a49bd: More than I could have done...
hcf2e7895g5f88g47b2g85ebg4d0e897815c4: Is that what Ethel told you?
ha24d3a95gcf10g46f3gb130g204f41e0f3f2: She said I'd make a bad mother. I... I think she was right.
h7208922fg4b38g4122ga9f2g5a9ef2216f11: Now I'll have to drag Connor's coffin all the way home. It's the only way this child will ever meet their father.
h645aceb9g33e4g4a0fga152ge599a4461968: Don't sell yourself short. You can raise this child.
h00fe588cg606dg4a58gbb13g64df1e0d42f2: I always wanted to be a mother. I... just never thought I'd do it alone.
hd9aa3afbg7bb1g4149g8ffeg4eee6ada0845: From what I've seen, you'd make a terrible mother.
ha2990eccgee2eg4b32gaed2g3bd460acf5f1: I... I think you're right. That's what Ethel always told me.
h411b02b7g61c6g4b7egb8e5g02138ceb47ec: You should have known better - hags don't help anyone but themselves.
hd2159bb8gaf89g4284ga085g133235bcce0c: Well, I didn't bloody ask you, did I? I didn't ask for any of this! I just wanted Connor back.
h304561degb566g4c7fga99fgf76b58ae47ef: With hags, nothing is ever over.
ha8a19de9g69b7g4fe7g9b00ga31582bf0d6e: This was the best chance I had - for me and my baby.
h78405c13g5f6fg4faeg8465gbbc79020a2f7: A life for a life - you must really love your husband.
ha96c0856g30b4g4ce1gbd61gc9def91d333a: Don't be naive - a hag was never going to help you.
h12c55a9bgcb4dg45c5gb514g1c11f5f30c7f: She would've! I had everything figured out.
h0d699bf7g303cg4ed0g8cd5g6f245b278dc0: Hags don't work for free. What did you promise her?
h8628fa62gcee9g4e2ega5a3gcf5e6c5b6585: No, I'm not! Why ruin my deal when I was so close?
h8c4a0fd4gc7eeg4475g9a29g1aeb95f65264: Auntie Ethel planned to eat your child.
h2e03b043gb0fdg482fgab1bgd39d6d5c21b7: What? But she promised to look after them! Teach them magic!
hd9c59964g4165g4923ga567g5d76d65cceb4: I'm sorry - she lied to you.
hcfec0a28g0b05g4b00g8b17g4d01da6622f7: No, you were desperate. And she took advantage of that.
h5b9c99f7gd77eg4bafg937ega24051a62ead: I thought I could fix it. Give my child a good home and bring my husband back - all in one go.
h4b3b1a48g924eg4d5eg90ffge0b7fc00a007: I'm sorry. You've been through a lot.
h7e624b13g4412g4419gaec3gf2fdd069357e: People always say that. It doesn't mean anything.
h01100285g4baeg49e5gbad3g73b9bbbfe311: I'm done. With you, with this place. After everything I went through to get Connor back.
h1d1db110g92c1g4560ga046g8aaa6380345a: Now I have to drag his corpse back home, coffin and all, because a goddamn grave is the closest I'll get to seeing him again.
h0ae26158g007bg4352g8ef1g8edba1a75482: Look, I - I don't have much...
h70cba8c1gdf85g4358g89b2g4db53fa01f10: ... but take this locket. It's worth a bit. Now we're even.
h19d6b69bg507bg4edcgbeddg5f8fd66041d2: Not much indeed. Got nothing else?
ha575a4dcg5791g4af7g856bg08db8aa568b1: It's this or the clothes off my back - and I'm not walking out of here starkers. You want it or not?
h8be7b13egc542g44c9g9ba7g3092ae7c16a9: Keep it. You don't owe me anything.
h07d988e9g2c78g4c1bg9066g0ad95fc6ae0f: My husband gave it to me. I should take him home.
hc3e5a35agb024g42acgb566gc117965b69f9: His coffin is just upstairs. A decent burial is the least I can give him.
had62edd3g0d60g40c1g92c6gfa5165c7468d: Thank you. I don't think there's anyone else who would've saved me.
h628cb720g96e7g45c0g9ccdg19c006db51f4: Just look after it, all right?
h086507c0g71e9g4976g8b41gb21dfd4dba4f: Now is the time to focus on your child.
h792d03a4g50f8g4ce2gbe95g8cc41123ebee: You think my baby ever left my mind? Not even at my lowest.
hf697dc18g060cg4b5dg9014g7bbe69827d4b: It was obviously too good to be true.
hecb07e12g292dg4cd8g8d89g1988bcf17bb2: I mean - turning a child into a hag does involve teaching them magic...
h8e0e73c5gf3a1g44e2ga921g9c7c0354c1c1: You're an idiot for believing that.
ha741160ag3866g4409g95b6g43fdd02dd05a: I'm sorry. I was trying to help.
h490e0c29gba15g4f93gaf7fg11e0d595f2b7: This is an interesting way of thanking me.
haef3420bg3120g436agab2cg3aec098180a3: You want thanks? A slap is all you deserve.
h49d567e2g5063g4f6fg94c7g8961e52d0f45: I don't need this. Good luck getting out of here on your own.
h1fcb971dg30c9g4e8ega8dfg904302bec77b: Go on. Walk away. And wave to my husband's rotting corpse on your way out.
h2fe193d6ge4eag4ca3g8766g8a728910aba7: That's right - he's buried outside. And I was this close to getting him back.
h2270bbb5g808cg40dcgb103gc94540d89437: Thanks to you, I'll never see him again.
